novel_features.name: Trademark One-Look Status
novel_features.description: Full current state of a trademark in one command — mark text, status, owner, classes, filing/registration dates, attorney, and prosecution event count
novel_features.rationale: The TSDR API returns complex XML/JSON with nested bags; this parses it into a clean single-screen snapshot that no existing tool offers
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents evaluating trademark status need the complete picture in one call instead of parsing XML manually

novel_features.name: Prosecution Timeline
novel_features.description: Every prosecution event in chronological order — office actions, examiner reviews, publication events, and registration milestones
novel_features.rationale: Prosecution history is buried in a nested bag inside the status response; this extracts and sorts all events chronologically
novel_features.why_it_matters: Trademark attorneys need the full event history to evaluate prosecution strength and identify potential issues

novel_features.name: Deadline Calculator
novel_features.description: Calculate Section 8, 9, and 15 maintenance deadlines with window-open dates and days-away countdown
novel_features.rationale: No existing tool computes trademark maintenance deadlines from registration dates; this requires domain knowledge about Section 8 (5-6yr, then 10yr), Section 9 (10yr), and Section 15 (5yr)
novel_features.why_it_matters: Missing a maintenance deadline means losing the registration — this is the #1 pain point for trademark portfolio managers

novel_features.name: Portfolio Watch
novel_features.description: Monitor multiple trademarks for status changes — caches previous statuses locally and flags any changes since last check
novel_features.rationale: Requires comparing current API results against locally cached previous statuses; SaaS competitors charge $100-$100K+/year for this
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents monitoring trademark portfolios need change detection, not full status dumps they have to diff themselves

novel_features.name: Batch Status Check
novel_features.description: Batch status lookup for multiple trademarks using the multi-case endpoint or individual fallback with rate-limit throttling
novel_features.rationale: Uses the caseMultiStatus endpoint for efficient batch lookups; falls back to individual requests with automatic throttling for large portfolios
novel_features.why_it_matters: IP paralegals managing hundreds of marks need batch status without manually checking each one

novel_features.name: Document Listing
novel_features.description: List all documents in the prosecution file — office actions, responses, specimens, registration certificates — with type and date filtering
novel_features.rationale: Document metadata is returned in a separate endpoint with its own nested structure; this parses and presents it as a sortable table
novel_features.why_it_matters: Litigation prep and due diligence require reviewing every document in a trademark file without clicking through the TSDR web UI

narrative.headline: The only CLI for USPTO trademark status, prosecution history, document retrieval, and deadline tracking — with portfolio monitoring no existing tool offers
narrative.value_prop: Look up trademark status, prosecution timelines, and documents from the USPTO TSDR API. Track maintenance deadlines, monitor portfolio changes, and batch-check marks — all with a local SQLite cache for offline search. Works with the TSDR Data API at tsdrapi.uspto.gov.
narrative.auth_narrative: Get a free API key from account.uspto.gov/api-manager (requires a MyUSPTO account). Note: TSDR uses a separate key from the patent Open Data Portal. Set it with `export TSDR_APIKEY_HEADER=your-key`. Run `doctor` to verify connectivity.

quickstart.comment: Verify your API key and TSDR connectivity
Look up a trademark by serial number

troubleshoots.fix: Set TSDR_APIKEY_HEADER — get a free key at account.uspto.gov/api-manager (requires MyUSPTO account). Note: this is a DIFFERENT key from the patent Open Data Portal.
TSDR rate limit is 60 req/min (120 off-peak). The CLI throttles automatically; if you see this, wait 60 seconds.

troubleshoots.symptom: 404 Not Found for a serial number
troubleshoots.fix: Serial numbers must be exactly 8 digits. The CLI auto-prepends 'sn' — enter just the digits (e.g., 97123456, not sn97123456).
The TSDR API defaults to XML. The CLI's novel commands (trademark status/timeline/etc.) use content negotiation to request JSON automatically.

narrative.when_to_use: Use the USPTO TSDR CLI when you need to check trademark application status, review prosecution history, download case documents, track maintenance deadlines, or monitor a portfolio of marks. Best for IP due diligence, trademark portfolio management, opposition monitoring, and deadline tracking. Ideal for agents that need structured trademark data without managing XML parsing or rate limits.

recipes.title: Check trademark status
recipes.explanation: Get the current state of a trademark with key fields

recipes.title: Review prosecution history
recipes.explanation: See every prosecution event in chronological order

recipes.title: Track maintenance deadlines
recipes.explanation: Calculate Section 8, 9, and 15 deadlines with days-away countdown

recipes.title: Monitor a portfolio
recipes.explanation: Check multiple marks and flag any status changes since last run
